raw_output_payload.reasoning:
We need to produce a bash command that does this. Input string: "1/2 [3] (27/03/2012 19:32:54) word word word word 4/5". Remove everything within parentheses. So remove "(27/03/2012 19:32:54)". Then substitute all non-digit characters with a space. So digits remain. After removal, string becomes "1/2 [3]  word word word word 4/5". Then replace non-digits with space: digits and spaces? Non-digit replaced by space. So digits remain, others become space. So "1/2 [3]  word word word word 4/5" -> digits and spaces: "1 2   3     4 5". Actually slash and brackets become spaces. So result: "1 2   3     4 5". Then format as a table. Maybe use column -t? So command: echo "1/2 [3] (27/03/2012 19:32:54) word word word word 4/5" | sed 's/([^)]*)//g' | tr -cd '0-9
' | tr -s '
' ' ' ??? Wait need spaces between numbers. Actually we want to replace non-digits with space: tr -cd '0-9
' would delete non-digits. But we need spaces where non-digits were. So use sed 's/[^0-9]/ /g'. Then pipe to column -t. So final command: echo "1/2 [3] (27/03/2012 19:32:54) word word word word 4/5" | sed 's/([^)]*)//g' | sed 's/[^0-9]/ /g' | column -t. Let's output that.
